quoteFirst: For example, a resident of Wahoo wishing to go to Omaha by train leaves at 11:15 A.M., there being only one train a day, arrives at Omaha at 1:15 P.M.; but must wait until the next day to return, as the only train leaves Omaha at
quoteLast: ; arriving at Wahoo at 2:31 P.M. The citizen has, therefore, spent practically two days to make the trip.
title: Highways and Highway Transportation
author: Chatburn, George R., 1863-1940

title: The Chautauquan, Vol. 04, June 1884, No. 9
